name: South Korea
summary: Day 73-74 (Mon-Tue). **KOSPI 7,690.59 May 12 (-1.7% 1D, +33.1% 1M, +39.3% 3M) — FIRST PROFIT-TAKING SESSION AFTER EXTRAORDINARY VERTICAL RUN.** USD/KRW ₩1,488.28 (+1.9% 1D) — won materially weakening on (i) HMM Namu attack on Korea-flagged vessel + (ii) KOSPI capital outflow. **B9 HMM NAMU FORENSICS PUBLIC MAY 11: Korean joint investigation team confirmed vessel struck May 4 anchored off UAE was hit by 2 unidentified aerial objects ~1 min apart at the stern, causing engine-room fire; MINE AND TORPEDO RULED OUT by damage pattern; engine debris recovered for forensic analysis. SEOUL STOPPED SHORT OF PUBLICLY BLAMING IRAN** — creating diplomatic friction with Trump, who had publicly attributed to Iran from the start. First commercial-vessel strike of cycle with conclusive public forensic investigation. Korea Times: 'may shift Seoul stance' on potential South Korean involvement in any Hormuz protection mission. **Korea has gone from 'passive helium victim' to 'active diplomatic actor' with HMM Namu forensics.** Won weakness suggests foreign capital trimming Korea exposure on diplomatic-friction risk while Korea-domestic capital absorbs at lower prices. KOSPI -1.7% on a 7,800-printing market is shallow — real test is whether 7,500 holds within 5 sessions. **Helium tail-risk timeline tightening: Korean fab buffer now ~4 WEEKS REMAINING to mid-June exhaustion.** Tier-1 chipmaker production-halt falsification still HOLDS but window contracting. SK hynix continues 'diversified supplies' (CLAIMED — one-sided); TrendForce 'two-week clock' (March 24) now ~7 weeks elapsed without confirmed halt. SMH $576.31 (+1.7% 1D, +31.9% 1M); AI infrastructure CapEx demand from Microsoft/Meta/Amazon unbroken. **STRUCTURAL ASSESSMENT (still most reversible position in world if helium buffers deplete past mid-June):** Crisis-linked share rising from ~5% (helium tail) to ~40% (helium + HMM Namu + diplomatic friction).
